cases.question: As of the 2020 census, what was the population difference between the largest county seat and smallest county seat, by land area of the county seat, in Washington state? For population figures, please use the official data from data.census…
cases.final_answer: The integer population difference between Washington’s most populous county seat (Seattle, with a 2020 census population of **737,015**) and its least populous county seat (Cathlamet, with a 2020 census population of **560**)—when ranked by the land area of their respective cities—is **736,455**. This result is derived from official 2020 U.S. Census data for these two cities, as reported on their Wikipedia pages (which cite the Census Bureau). While many Washington county seats are small towns, Seattle stands out as both the largest city by land area (83.99 sq mi) and the most populous county seat, while Cathlamet—though geographically compact (0.51 sq mi)—remains the smallest-populated county seat.

cases.question: 在2020年夏季奥运会中有一对参与同一届奥运国家游泳队的姐妹，请问这对姐妹的姐姐和妹妹年龄差多少周？（精确到个位）
cases.final_answer:
The age difference between the two sisters is approximately **103 weeks** (approximately 103.4 weeks). To be precise:  
- Cate Campbell was born on **20 May 1992**.  
- Bronte Campbell was born on **14 May 1994**.  
- The absolute time difference between these dates is **724 days**.  
- Converting this to weeks gives **724 ÷ 7 ≈ 103.42857 weeks**, which rounds to **103 weeks** when expressed to the nearest whole number (i.e., "exact to the nearest week").

Thus, the older sister (Cate) and the younger sister (Bronte) differ in age by roughly **103 weeks**.

cases.question: 谁是山东财经大学计算机与人工智能学院特聘教授中，最早获得国家级荣誉的人？
cases.final_answer: The earliest nationally recognized honor among the specially appointed professors (特聘教授) in the School of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at Shandong University of Finance and Economics belongs to **Wang Meng** (汪萌). She is a specially appointed professor at the university, and her first major national-level honor was receiving the **National Natural Science Foundation of China’s Outstanding Youth Fund** (awarded in 2013), which qualifies as a significant national-level recognition. While other specially appointed professors like Zhang Caiming (张彩明) also hold national honors (e.g., State Council Special Subsidy and Ministry of Education Science and Technology Progress Awards), Wang Meng’s early achievement—her receipt of the NNSFC Outstanding Youth Fund in 2013—makes her the earliest among those listed.
